1.3: Saint Paul is speaking here of certain ancient heretics such as the Encratites, the Ebionites, the Manicheans, etc., who maintained that marriage was forbidden as an impure thing, while they themselves permitted the community of women and all the horrors that it entails; and who moreover defended the use of meat, claiming that it came from the principle of evil.
1.13: Christ Jesus, who bore such a beautiful testimony, that is, who confirmed his preaching by his testimony. Cf. Matthew
1.20: Hymen and Alexander had been excommunicated by Saint Paul. Hymen is mentioned again, see
1.14: the opponent . It is the demon, as the following verse seems to indicate. Cf.
1.5: Although Jesus Christ is the sole mediator of redemption, one can have recourse to the prayers and intercession of the faithful on earth, and of the angels and saints in heaven, to obtain mercy, grace and salvation through Jesus Christ, as Saint Paul himself asks for the help of the prayers of the faithful, without doing any injure to the mediation of Jesus Christ.
1.12: Fight the good fight ; that is to say, valiantly support the good fight; a kind of repetition which aims to give strength and energy to the speech.
1.24: The Apostle means that there are some men whose sins are already known, before the examination that could be made and the judgment that could be made, while there are others whose faults are only discovered as a result of this examination.

2.1: First of all, I urge that prayers, supplications, intercessions, and thanksgivings be made for all people,
2.2: for kings and for those in authority, so that we may live peaceful and quiet lives in all godliness and holiness.
2.3: This is good and pleasing in the sight of God our Savior,
2.4: who wants all men to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.
2.5: For there is one God and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus,
2.6: who gave himself as a ransom for all: this is a fact attested in its time,
2.7: And it is for this purpose that I was appointed a preacher and an apostle; I speak the truth, I am not lying, a teacher of the nations in faith and truth.
2.8: Therefore I want men to pray everywhere, raising pure hands to heaven, without anger or agitation of thought.
2.9: Likewise, women should dress modestly, adorning themselves with decency and simplicity, without braids, gold, pearls, or sumptuous garments, but with good works,
2.10: as befits women who profess to serve God.
2.11: Let the woman receive instruction in silence, with complete submission.
2.12: I do not permit a woman to teach, nor to assume authority over a man, but she must remain silent.
2.13: For Adam was formed first, then Eve
2.14: And it was not Adam who was seduced, but the woman who, seduced, fell into transgression.
2.15: Nevertheless, she will be saved by becoming a mother, provided she perseveres in faith, charity, and holiness, united with modesty.

3.1: This saying is certain: if anyone aspires to the office of bishop, he desires an excellent office.
3.2: Therefore, the bishop must be irreproachable, have had only one wife, be sober, prudent, well-mannered, welcoming, and capable of teaching.,
3.3: that he be neither given to wine, nor violent, but gentle, peaceable, and selfless,
3.4: that he governs his own house well and keeps his children submissive, with perfect honesty.
3.5: For if someone does not know how to manage his own household, how will he take care of God's church?
3.6: Let it not be a new convert, lest, becoming puffed up with pride, he fall into the same condemnation as the devil.
3.7: He must also enjoy the consideration of those outside, so as not to fall into disgrace and the traps of the devil.
3.8: Likewise, let the deacons be worthy men, not duplicitous in their speech, nor given to wine, nor greedy for sordid gain.
3.9: but that they preserve the mystery of faith in a pure conscience.
3.10: Let them first be tested and then let them carry out their ministry, if they are found without fault.
3.11: Women, likewise, must be honorable, not slanderous, temperate, faithful in all things.
3.12: Deacons should be husbands of one wife, and should govern their children and their own household well.
3.13: For those who fulfill their ministry well obtain an honorable standing and great confidence in the faith of Jesus Christ.
3.14: I am writing these things to you, although I hope to come to you soon.,
3.15: so that, if I am delayed, you may know how you ought to conduct yourself in the house of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and foundation of the truth.
3.16: And undoubtedly, this is a great mystery of godliness, he who was manifested in the flesh, justified in the Spirit, beheld by angels, preached among the nations, believed on in the world, exalted in glory.

4.1: But the Spirit clearly says that in times to come some will abandon the faith and follow deceiving spirits and demonic teachings.,
4.2: taught by hypocritical imposters who bear the mark of disgrace on their own conscience,
4.3: who forbid marriage and the use of foods that God created so that the faithful and those who have known the truth may use them with thanksgiving.
4.4: For everything God created is good, and nothing should be rejected if it is received with thanksgiving.,
4.5: because everything is sanctified by the word of God and by prayer.
4.6: By offering these teachings to the brothers, you will be a good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished by the lessons of faith and good doctrine which you have faithfully followed.
4.7: As for these profane fables, these old wives' tales, reject them and practice piety.
4.8: For physical exercise is somewhat useful, but piety is useful in all things, holding promise for this life and for the life to come.
4.9: This is a certain and worthy statement.
4.10: for we do not take so much trouble and do not endure insults except because we have placed our hope in the living God who is the Savior of all men, especially of the faithful.
4.11: This is what you must prescribe and teach.
4.12: Let no one despise you because of your youth, but set an example to the believers in speech, in conduct, in love, in faith, in chastity.
4.13: Until I come, devote yourself to reading, exhorting, and teaching.
4.14: Do not neglect the grace that is in you, which was given you through prophetic action when the assembly of elders laid hands on you.
4.15: Meditate on these things and be fully present in them, so that your progress may be evident to all.
4.16: Watch over yourself and your teaching, apply yourself constantly because, by doing so, you will save yourself and those who listen to you.

5.1: Do not rebuke an old man harshly, but admonish him as a father, and young men as brothers,
5.2: The older women are treated like mothers, the younger ones like sisters, with complete purity.
5.3: Honor the widows who are truly widows.
5.4: If a widow has children or grandchildren, let them first learn to practice piety towards their own family and to give back to their parents what they have received from them.
5.5: As for the one who is truly a widow, who is alone in the world, she has put her hope in God and she perseveres night and day in supplications and prayers.
5.6: For the one who lives in pleasure, she is dead, although she appears alive.
5.7: Give them these recommendations, so that they will be beyond reproach.
5.8: But if anyone does not provide for his relatives, especially those of his family, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
5.9: A widow, to be registered on the list, must be at least sixty years old and have been the wife of only one husband.,
5.10: that she has a reputation for her good works, having: raised her children, practiced hospitality, washed the feet of the saints, helped the unfortunate, and undertaken all kinds of good works.
5.11: As for young widows, keep them away, for when the allure of pleasure turns them away from Christ, they want to remarry.
5.12: and make themselves guilty by failing to fulfill their initial commitment.
5.13: Moreover, in their idleness, they get used to going from house to house and not only are they idle, but also talkative, curious, talking about things that should not be talked about.
5.14: Therefore, I desire that young widows marry, have children, manage their households, and give their adversaries no occasion for slander.,
5.15: because there are already some who have gone astray to follow Satan.
5.16: If any faithful man or woman has widows in his family, let him provide for their needs and let the Church not be overburdened, so that it may assist those who are truly widows.
5.17: Elders who govern well are worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in preaching and teaching.,
5.18: For the Scripture says, «You shall not muzzle an ox while it treads out the grain,» and, «The worker deserves his wages.»
5.19: It does not accept accusations against an elder, except on the testimony of two or three witnesses.
5.20: Those who fail in their duties, rebuke them in front of everyone, so as to inspire fear in the others.
5.21: I adjure you before God, before Christ Jesus, and before the elect angels, to observe these things impartially and to do nothing out of favoritism.
5.22: Do not lay hands on anyone too quickly, and do not share in the sins of others; keep yourself pure.
5.23: Don't continue to drink only water, but have some wine because of your stomach and your frequent ailments.
5.24: There are people whose sins are obvious, even before they are judged, but others are only discovered afterwards.
5.25: Similarly, good works are obvious, and those that are not obvious at first cannot remain hidden.

6.1: Let all who are under the yoke as slaves consider their masters worthy of all honor so that the name of God and his doctrine may not be blasphemed.
6.2: And let those who have believers as their masters not despise them because they are their brothers, but let them serve them all the better, since those who receive their service are brothers and friends. This is what must be taught and recommended.
6.3: If anyone teaches a different doctrine and does not adhere to the saving words of our Lord Jesus Christ and to the teaching that conforms to godliness,
6.4: He is a proud, ignorant, and deranged man who occupies himself with questions and disputes over words, from which arise envy, quarrels, insults, and malicious suspicions.,
6.5: the endless discussions of men who have a perverted mind, who, deprived of the truth, see in piety only a means of enriching themselves.
6.6: Indeed, piety content with what is necessary is a great wealth.,
6.7: because we brought nothing into the world and undoubtedly we can take nothing out of it.
6.8: So if we have enough to eat and enough to wear, we will be satisfied.
6.9: Those who want to be rich fall into temptation, a trap, and a host of foolish and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction.
6.10: For the love of money is the root of all kinds of evil, and some people, eager for money, have wandered from the faith and pierced themselves with many griefs.
6.11: But you, man of God, flee from these desires, and pursue instead justice, godliness, faith, love, patience, and gentleness.
6.12: Fight the good fight of faith, conquer the eternal life to which you were called and for which you made this beautiful confession of faith before a great number of witnesses.
6.13: I commend you before God, who gives life to all things, and before Christ Jesus, who bore such a good testimony under Pontius Pilate,
6.14: to keep the commandment spotless and blameless until the manifestation of our Lord Jesus Christ,
6.15: which the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of kings and Lord of lords, will reveal in his time,
6.16: who alone possesses immortality, who dwells in unapproachable light, whom no man has seen nor can see, to whom belong honor and eternal power, amen.
6.17: Instruct those who are rich in this present world not to be arrogant, nor to put their hope in wealth, which is so uncertain, but in God, who richly provides us with everything we need for life.,
6.18: to do good, to become rich in good works, to be quick to give generously of what they have,
6.19: thus accumulating a solid treasure for the future that will allow them to acquire true life.
6.20: O Timothy, guard the deposit, avoiding vain and profane talk and all that opposes a science that does not deserve the name,
6.21: Some, by professing this, have erred in their faith. Grace be with you, amen.
